title: jacobaea kirghisica
intro: Jacobaea kirghisica, also known as Khel or Jacobaea, is a plant species in the sunflower family (Asteraceae) native to Central Asia. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic effects. The health benefits of Jacobaea kirghisica include its ability to treat various ailments, such as wounds, ulcers, and skin conditions, due to its antibacterial and antifungal properties. Jacobaea kirghisica is often prepared as a tincture or infusion in herbal medicine, and its roots and leaves are used to make medicinal preparations.
